summary: Iter 69 bulk payslips + annual report — Backend-only testing of 3 new endpoints (payslips.pdf, payslips.zip, reports/annual.xlsx). Created new test file test_iter69_bulk_payslips_and_annual.py with 20 tests: 18 PASSED, 2 skipped due to environment data limitations (no salary run with non-zero rows exists in this preview env). All acceptance criteria met: (1) payslips.pdf returns application/pdf with %PDF- magic, .pdf in Content-Disposition, 404 for unknown run, empty-run yields placeholder PDF (not 500), cross-firm 403 works; (2) payslips.zip returns application/zip with PK magic, cross-firm 403 works, 404 for unknown run; (3) annual.xlsx returns proper XLSX with PK magic and all 4 required sheets (Summary, Salary — per employee, Attendance, PF & ESIC), 400 without company_id, 404 for bogus company_id, company_admin auto-forced to own firm regardless of query param. Also added unit-level tests hitting build_bulk_payslip_pdf / build_payslip_pdf / build_annual_report_xlsx directly to lock in behavior even when env has no rows. Iter-68 regression suite (16 tests) + iter-69 empty-rows unit (2 tests) still all PASS.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:10865-10979 — Both bulk endpoints correctly read `run.get('rows') or []` (matching the iter-68 field-name fix); no reintroduction of the 'lines' bug. Auth guard uses run.company_id != admin.company_id which correctly rejects both foreign-firm AND null-company runs for company_admin. Good defensive coding.

server.py:10982-11026 — /admin/reports/annual.xlsx correctly forces admin.company_id for company_admin BEFORE the 400/404 checks, so a company_admin passing a foreign company_id neither errors nor leaks foreign data — they silently get their own firm's report. Verified in test_company_admin_scoped_to_own_firm.

utils/payslip_pdf.py:495-500 — Empty entries fall through to a single-paragraph placeholder page so ReportLab still gets flowables and doesn't raise LayoutError. Same pattern as iter-68 salary_register_pdf empty-rows guard. Nice consistency.

utils/annual_report.py:120-146 — Uses .to_list(50) for salary_runs / comp_runs which caps at 50. Since FY has 12 months and typically ≤5 firms per run, 50 is safe for now but should be increased or removed if a firm ever accumulates more historical runs.

Suggestion (LOW): payslips.zip iterates rows and calls build_payslip_pdf() sequentially. For firms with hundreds of employees this is O(N) blocking work on the event loop. Consider running the PDF generation in a threadpool (`await asyncio.to_thread(...)`) to keep the async server responsive. Non-blocking.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iter69_bulk_payslips_and_annual.py (NEW — 20 tests covering all 3 endpoints + utility builders)

success_rate.backend: 100% pass rate (18/18 executed; 2 skipped due to env data — no live salary run has non-empty rows in this preview env)

seed_data_creation: No persistent seed data. Session fixtures only read the existing salary runs / companies. The one existing salary run (srun_c0abeceedc, month=2026-07, 0 rows, company_id=None) was reused for empty-run PDF placeholder verification and cross-firm 403 checks (its null company_id causes company_admin.company_id != None → 403 which matches the guard's intent).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter-69 bulk payslips + annual report endpoints all verified. Two tests are skipped in current env: test_company_admin_download_own_firm and test_zip_contents_match_rows_and_naming — both require a salary run scoped to SKSCO1 with at least one row containing user_id. In current preview env the ONLY salary run has company_id=None and 0 rows. If main agent later seeds a real SKSCO1 salary run (via existing /admin/salary-runs/generate flow), rerun `pytest backend/tests/test_iter69_bulk_payslips_and_annual.py -v` and the two skips will auto-convert to PASS (they already handle the fixture-populated path). To rerun everything for iter-68+69: `pytest backend/tests/test_iter68_admin_employee_and_pdfs.py backend/tests/test_iter69_salary_register_empty_rows.py backend/tests/test_iter69_bulk_payslips_and_annual.py -v`.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no bugs found in iter-69 endpoints. All three new routes behave exactly as specified: correct content-types, magic bytes, 4-sheet workbook, company-admin scoping, empty-run placeholder, 400/404 error paths, and auth guards.
